name: ubik
title: Ubik
description: Psssst! — an AI resident engineer for your codebase. Audits the repo, surfaces stale code / missing tests / inconsistent patterns / security smells into a structured markdown report, and keeps a searchable notebook of past findings. Pair the MCP surface with the autonomous daemon (`ubik run`) to also get Telegram-approved fixes shipped through Aider or the Claude Agent SDK.

tools.name: ubik.audit
tools.description: Single-shot audit of a local repository. Reads the codebase, runs the researcher loop with extended thinking, writes a structured markdown report (TL;DR · findings with severity / evidence / fix / risk / ETA · open questions) into Ubik's notebook, and returns the report inline.

input.path: string (required) — absolute or relative path to the repository
input.project_name: string (optional) — override project name on the report
input.max_tokens: integer (optional, default 8000) — cap on the model reply

tools.name: ubik.notebook.recent
tools.description: Return the N most recent notebook entries, newest first. Each carries title, summary, kind (audit / proposal / research), severity, tags, and a stable slug.

tools.name: ubik.notebook.search
tools.description: Substring search across past notebook entries' titles, summaries, and tags. Returns matches newest-first.

input.kind: string (optional) — one of audit / daily / weekly / monthly / proposal / research

tools.name: ubik.notebook.read
tools.description: Fetch the full markdown body of a single notebook entry by slug. Use after `ubik.notebook.recent` or `ubik.notebook.search` to read details.

prompts.name: ubik-audit
prompts.description: Run an Ubik audit on a repo path and summarise the TL;DR + highest-severity finding.

prompts.name: ubik-recent
prompts.description: Show recent Ubik notebook entries grouped by project.

prompts.name: ubik-research
prompts.description: Run an audit focused on a specific topic against the current repo.

_notes.transports: stdio only in 0.1.5. Streamable-HTTP + OAuth 2.1 are on the roadmap (see docs/roadmap.md, Sprint 2.4).
_notes.resources: Not yet exposed — `ubik.notebook.read` returns the full body inline as text content. Resource URI templates land when HTTP transport ships.
_notes.honesty: Every tool, prompt, and transport listed here is implemented and registered with the MCP server in `ubik/mcp/server.py`. Earlier card revisions advertised tools / resources / oauth that didn't exist; this version matches the code.
